evals.prompt: I have a Product Owner Specification at /home/shadowx4fox/Project/Personal/solutions-architect-skills/architecture-docs-workspace/evals/files/PRODUCT_OWNER_SPEC.md for the PayStream payment processing platform. Please create the full architecture documentation for this system. Use a Microservices architecture type.
evals.expected_output: Full multi-file architecture docs created: ARCHITECTURE.md (nav index), docs/01-system-overview.md through docs/09-operational-considerations.md, with content derived from the PO Spec. Correct structure, section content, and PO Spec traceability.
expectations: Creates ARCHITECTURE.md as a navigation index (not monolithic) with links to docs/ files
Creates docs/01-system-overview.md with content from the PO Spec (mentions PayStream, 500,000 transactions/day, 99.99% availability)
Creates docs/07-security-architecture.md for S9 Security (not docs/09-*)
Creates docs/09-operational-considerations.md for S11 Operational (not docs/11-*)
PO Spec traceability comment is embedded in docs/01-system-overview.md
Key metrics from PO Spec appear in the system overview (P99 ≤ 2s, 99.99% availability, 500k TPS)
PCI-DSS compliance requirements are reflected in the security section
Architecture type metadata comment is present in docs/03-architecture-layers.md

evals.prompt: I'm working on the PayStream architecture at /home/shadowx4fox/Project/Personal/solutions-architect-skills/architecture-docs-workspace/evals/files/eval-1-section-update/. Update Section 9 (Security Architecture) to replace the current API key authentication with OAuth2 using PKCE flow for the API Gateway. Access tokens should expire in 15 minutes, refresh tokens in 7 days. The Authorization Server should be AWS Cognito.
evals.expected_output: The file docs/07-security-architecture.md is updated (not docs/09-*) with OAuth2/PKCE details, token expiry values, and AWS Cognito as the auth server. Existing PCI-DSS content preserved.
expectations: Edits docs/07-security-architecture.md (correct file for S9), NOT docs/09-operational-considerations.md
The updated security section mentions OAuth2 and PKCE
Access token expiry of 15 minutes is documented
Refresh token expiry of 7 days is documented
AWS Cognito is named as the Authorization Server
Existing PCI-DSS controls table is preserved in the file
Does NOT create a new file — edits the existing docs/07-security-architecture.md

evals.prompt: Looking at our PayStream architecture at /home/shadowx4fox/Project/Personal/solutions-architect-skills/architecture-docs-workspace/evals/files/eval-2-query/ — can you explain how the checkout flow works? What happens step by step when a customer makes a payment?
evals.expected_output: A clear explanation of the checkout flow sourced from docs/04-data-flow-patterns.md (S6), covering the 6-step sequence from API Gateway through authorization to confirmation, with specific details like Kafka topic names, VGS tokenization, and latency target.
expectations: Answer is sourced from docs/04-data-flow-patterns.md (S6 Data Flow), not from docs/06-* or other files
Response covers all 6 steps of the checkout flow (Validation, VGS tokenization, Authorization, Kafka publish, Ledger write, Confirmation)
VGS vault tokenization step is mentioned (PAN is never stored)
Kafka topic 'payment.authorized' is referenced in the explanation
P99 ≤ 2 second latency target is mentioned
Error handling or saga/outbox pattern is noted (e.g., rollback on Kafka failure)
